questions.question: How many primitive data types does Java have?
Which of the following is NOT a primitive data type in Java?
What is the size of an int in Java?
What is the range of the byte data type in Java?
Which suffix must be appended to a numeric literal to make it a long value?
Which suffix must be appended to a numeric literal to make it a float value?
What is the size of a double in Java?
What kind of quotes does a char literal use in Java?
What does it mean that Java is a statically typed language?
options: Variables cannot change value once assigned
Every variable must have a declared type before it can be used
Variables are always stored on the stack
questions.answer: Every variable must have a declared type before it can be used

questions.question: What is the difference between declaring a variable and initializing it?
options: Declaring specifies the type and name; initializing assigns it a value
Declaring assigns a value; initializing specifies the type
Declaring creates an object; initializing calls the constructor
questions.answer: Declaring specifies the type and name; initializing assigns it a value

questions.question: What happens if you try to use a local variable in Java without initializing it?
What does the final keyword do when applied to a variable?
options: Prevents the variable from being reassigned after initialization
Prevents the variable from being printed
questions.answer: Prevents the variable from being reassigned after initialization

questions.question: Which of the following correctly declares a final constant in Java?
What happens when you try to reassign a value to a final variable?
options: The new value is ignored silently

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid way to declare multiple variables of the same type on one line?
options: int a; int b; int c;

questions.question: What is the size of a short in Java?
What happens when you add 1 to Byte.MAX_VALUE and cast the result back to byte?
Approximately how many decimal digits of precision does a float provide?
Approximately how many decimal digits of precision does a double provide?
What happens if you try to assign a double value to an int variable without casting?
options: The int is promoted to double

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct way to declare and initialize a long variable?
options: All of the above are valid
questions.answer: All of the above are valid

questions.question: What are the only two values a boolean variable can hold in Java?
Why does Java provide multiple integer types (byte, short, int, long) instead of just one?
options: To make the language more confusing
To allow developers to use memory efficiently based on the range of values needed
Because each type supports different arithmetic operations
Because older versions of Java only had byte and short
questions.answer: To allow developers to use memory efficiently based on the range of values needed

questions.question: Which wrapper class constant would you use to find the maximum value of an int?
Is integer overflow a compile-time error or a runtime behavior in Java?
options: It is a runtime behavior that wraps around silently
It throws an ArithmeticException at runtime
It causes the program to terminate immediately
questions.answer: It is a runtime behavior that wraps around silently
